records.ety: [Cf. OE. fluschen to fly up, penetrate, F. fluz a flowing, E. flux, dial. Sw. flossa to blaze, and E. flash; perh. influenced by blush. √84.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Flushed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Flushing.]

defs: To flow and spread suddenly; to rush; as, blood flushes into the face.
quotes: The flushing noise of many waters.
It flushes violently out of the cock.

defs: To become suddenly suffused, as the cheeks; to turn red; to blush.
To snow red; to shine suddenly; to glow.
quotes: In her cheek, distemper flushing glowed.

defs: To start up suddenly; to take wing as a bird.
quotes: Flushing from one spray unto another.

defs: To cause to be full; to flood; to overflow; to overwhelm with water; as, to flush the meadows; to flood for the purpose of cleaning; as, to flush a sewer.
To cause the blood to rush into (the face); to put to the blush, or to cause to glow with excitement.
quotes: Nor flush with shame the passing virgin's cheek.
Sudden a thought came like a full-blown rose, Flushing his brow.

defs: To make suddenly or temporarily red or rosy, as if suffused with blood.
quotes: How faintly flushed. how phantom fair, Was Monte Rosa, hanging there!

defs: To excite; to animate; to stir.
quotes: Such things as can only feed his pride and flush his ambition.

defs: To cause to start, as a hunter a bird.
To cause to flow; to draw water from, or pour it over or through (a pond, meadow, sewer, etc.); to cleanse by means of a rush of water.
To operate a placer mine, where the continuous supply of water is insufficient, by holding back the water, and releasing it periodically in a flood.
To fill underground spaces, especially in coal mines, with material carried by water, which, after drainage, constitutes a compact mass.

records.ety: [From F. or Sp. flux. Cf. Flux.]

defs: A sudden flowing; a rush which fills or overflows, as of water for cleansing purposes.
quotes: In manner of a wave or flush.

defs: A suffusion of the face with blood, as from fear, shame, modesty, or intensity of feeling of any kind; a blush; a glow.
Any tinge of red color like that produced on the cheeks by a sudden rush of blood; as, the flush on the side of a peach; the flush on the clouds at sunset.
A sudden flood or rush of feeling; a thrill of excitement. animation, etc.; as, a flush of joy.
A flock of birds suddenly started up or flushed.

A hand of cards, all of the same suit; -- especially significant in poker, where five cards of the same suit constitute a flush, which beats a straight but is beaten by a full house or four of a kind.
Full of vigor; fresh; glowing; bright.
quotes: With all his crimes broad blown, as flush as May.

defs: Affluent; abounding; well furnished or supplied; hence, liberal; prodigal.
quotes: Lord Strut was not very flush in ready.

defs: Unbroken or even in surface; on a level with the adjacent surface; forming a continuous surface; as, a flush panel; a flush joint.
Consisting of cards of one suit.
So as to be level or even.
